meaning: n.可传送性,可遗传性,变速性
examples: There are two crucial factors: the transmissibility of the virus, and its virulence.
The virus can improve its transmissibility among humans via two principal mechanisms.
The Science study also provided the first estimate of the person-to-person transmissibility of H1N1.
Further research needed to establish likelihood of transmissibility of disease by public access to the countryside.
If H5N1 ever acquired this kind of transmissibility, he says, it probably could not be stopped without a vaccine.
